Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), mild metabolic acidosis (low CO₂), and abnormal liver enzymes (elevated ALT, low ALP). Urine findings (glucosuria, ketonuria, proteinuria, brown color) suggest possible diabetic ketoacidosis or nephrotic syndrome. Further clinical correlation and urgent nephrology and endocrinology evaluation are recommended.
